name: ManyChat API
what_it_does: Conversational marketing platform with REST API for building chatbots on Facebook Messenger, Instagram DMs, and WhatsApp — automating customer conversations, lead capture, and e-commerce flows with subscriber management and broadcast messaging.
use_cases: Managing subscribers and custom fields programmatically via REST API for CRM sync
Triggering ManyChat flows from external systems via API to automate messaging
Sending broadcast messages to subscriber segments from external workflows
Adding/removing subscriber tags based on external events (purchases, signups)
Looking up subscriber data and conversation state for multi-system automation
not_for: Business SMS at scale (ManyChat focuses on social messaging channels)
Complex support ticket management (use Zendesk or Freshdesk)
Building custom chatbot logic from scratch (ManyChat is a no-code/low-code platform)
best_when: A marketing team needs to connect ManyChat's subscriber database with external systems — syncing purchase events, CRM data, and triggering automated messaging flows.
avoid_when: You need raw WhatsApp API access, complex multi-agent customer support, or pure SMS marketing.

auth.notes: API token from account settings. Bearer token in Authorization header. Account-scoped access. No fine-grained scopes. Single token per ManyChat account. Page ID required for some operations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 1,000 contacts, basic features
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro from $15/month; scales by contact count
pricing.notes: API access available on Pro plan. Free tier for small audiences. US-based. Strong e-commerce integration ecosystem. WhatsApp channel requires Meta approval. Widely used by DTC brands for Messenger marketing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per account; not publicly documented in detail

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers subscriber management, custom fields, tags, and flow triggering. Swagger documentation available. Webhook support for subscriber events. API is supplementary to the visual flow builder. Limited to subscriber/contact operations — cannot create flows via API.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Subscriber lookup idempotent by channel user ID. Tag operations idempotent. Custom field updates idempotent. Flow trigger creates new execution — not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: API access requires Pro plan — not available on free tier
Cannot create or edit flows via API — visual builder only
WhatsApp requires Meta Business Manager approval — separate setup from API
Subscriber lookup requires channel-specific user ID (PSID for Messenger, etc.)

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. US data residency. API token auth. Webhook HMAC verification available. No fine-grained scopes. Meta platform policies add external security layer. No formal certifications beyond GDPR.
